docs.text:
Subject: Action Required: Data Export Fields Needed for Chat Intelligence Report (Power BI)

To: IT Admin / Global Admin
Re: Copilot Chat Intelligence – Power BI Report Setup


WHAT THIS REPORT DOES

The Chat Intelligence Report is a Power BI report that analyzes Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat usage across your organization. It segments users by engagement level (light to habitual), tracks session and prompt volume over time, and identifies adoption hotspots and licensing readiness signals by surface, org unit, and quartile.


DATA SOURCES REQUIRED

1. Microsoft Purview – Copilot Audit Logs
   Export: Purview portal (security.microsoft.com) -> Audit -> Activities: "Interacted with Copilot" -> Export, or PAX PowerShell script
   Format: CSV

2. Microsoft Entra ID – User/Org Data (includes license data)
   Export: entra.microsoft.com -> Identity -> Users -> Download users, or PAX script with -IncludeUserInfo
   Format: CSV

3. (if not using PAX) Microsoft 365 Admin Center – Licensed Users
   Export: admin.microsoft.com -> Reports -> Usage -> M365 Copilot -> Readiness tab -> Export
   Format: CSV

Note on license data: The report requires a hasLicense flag to separate licensed from unlicensed Copilot Chat usage. The PAX script automatically adds this column to the Entra user export — no separate file needed. If you are exporting manually from Entra, you will also need the M365 Admin Center licensed users file (source #3) provided as a separate input.


REQUIRED FIELDS — DO NOT REMOVE

IMPORTANT: If you are running a PAX Purview agent extract or manually exporting from Purview, do not remove or rename any columns from the output. Missing fields cause blank visuals with no error message.

Purview Audit Log (CopilotInteraction – Record Type 61):
CreationDate, UserId, Operations, AppHost, ThreadId, AgentId, Message_isPrompt.

Microsoft Entra ID – User Export:
UserPrincipalName, Department, hasLicense (added automatically by PAX; or use the M365 Admin Center licensed users file if exporting manually).

M365 Admin Center – Licensed Users (manual export path only):
UserPrincipalName, Has Copilot License Assigned.


INSIGHTS YOU WILL GAIN

- User engagement segmentation: light, moderate, regular, habitual Copilot Chat users
- Week-over-week session and prompt volume trends
- Surface breakdown: where chat usage is concentrated (BizChat, Teams, M365 apps)
- Licensed vs. unlicensed chat usage patterns
- Agent-assisted vs. standard chat session split
- Org-level adoption comparison by department or quartile


ROLES & PERMISSIONS REQUIRED

Export Purview audit logs: Audit Reader or Compliance Administrator
Export Entra user data (includes hasLicense via PAX): User Administrator or Global Reader
Export M365 Admin Center licensed users (manual path only): Global Administrator or Reports Reader
Run PAX script (optional automation): Audit Reader + AuditLog.Read.All, User.Read.All (Microsoft Graph)


SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS

- Power BI Desktop — required to open the .pbit template file
- PowerShell 5.1+ — required only if using the PAX automated export script
- Access to: security.microsoft.com, admin.microsoft.com, entra.microsoft.com


Note on Usernames:
Ensure user display names are not concealed in M365 Admin Center reports before exporting. Go to Settings -> Org Settings -> Services -> Reports and uncheck "Display concealed user, group, and site names in all reports."

Note on Hash String:
When opening the Power BI template, you will be prompted for a hash string. Provide any text string — this is used to de-identify sensitive user identifiers in the report output before sharing.

docs.title: Admin email template — 04_Super_User_Adoption_Admin_Email
docs.text:
Subject: Action Required: Viva Insights Query Setup Needed for Super User Adoption Report (Power BI)

To: IT Admin / Global Admin / Viva Insights Administrator
Re: Super User Adoption (DecodingSuperUsage) – Power BI Report Setup


WHAT THIS REPORT DOES

The Super User Adoption Report is a Power BI report powered by Viva Insights data. It identifies how Copilot super users emerge in your organization — tracking the journey from activation to habitual use — and helps you understand what work patterns, collaboration behaviors, and surfaces distinguish super users from the rest. Used to replicate adoption paths and focus enablement on the right cohorts.


DATA SOURCE REQUIRED

Viva Insights – Person Query
Export: analysis.insights.cloud.microsoft -> Create analysis -> Person query
Format: CSV or Direct Query (via Partition ID + Query ID)


REQUIRED FIELDS — DO NOT REMOVE

IMPORTANT: The Viva Insights Person Query must include ALL metrics listed below. Missing even one metric group will cause blank visuals in Power BI with no error. Do not export a partial query or remove columns from the CSV after export.

Person Query — Required Metric Groups (select "All metrics" for each):
Microsoft 365 Copilot (all metrics), Collaboration network (all metrics), Working hours collaboration (all metrics), Focus metrics (all metrics).

Specific fields the report depends on:
Copilot_Active_Use_Days_per_week, Copilot_Total_Actions, Copilot_Total_Chats, Copilot_Total_Emails_with_Copilot, Copilot_Total_Documents_with_Copilot, Copilot_Total_Meeting_Summaries, Copilot_Total_Pages_with_Copilot, Copilot_Total_Teams_Chat_Copilot_Interactions, Copilot_Total_Word_Copilot_Interactions, Copilot_Total_Excel_Copilot_Interactions, Copilot_Total_PowerPoint_Copilot_Interactions, Copilot_Total_Outlook_Copilot_Interactions, Collaboration_hours, Meeting_hours, After_hours_collaboration_hours, Email_hours, Focus_hours, Uninterrupted_focus_hours, Internal_network_size, Strong_ties, Diverse_ties.

Person Query — Required Attributes (Dimensions):
PersonId, Organization, FunctionType, TimeZone, IsActive.

Query Configuration — Required Settings:
- Time period: Last 6 months (rolling)
- Group by: Week (not Month — month grouping breaks trend calculations)
- Filter: Is Active = True


INSIGHTS YOU WILL GAIN

- Super user identification and tier classification (light to super user)
- Activation-to-habit journey: how fast users become super users and whether the behavior is durable
- Surface breakdown: which Copilot apps (Word, Excel, Teams, Outlook, Chat) super users engage with most
- Work pattern comparison: collaboration hours, meeting hours, focus time, and network size for super users vs. peers
- Team concentration: where super users are clustered, to focus enablement where momentum already exists


ROLES & PERMISSIONS REQUIRED

Create and run Person Query in Viva Insights: Insights Analyst (assigned in Viva Insights Admin)
Access Viva Insights Analyst Workbench: Insights Analyst or Insights Administrator
Export query results as CSV: Insights Analyst


SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS

- Power BI Desktop — required to open the .pbit template file
- Access to: analysis.insights.cloud.microsoft (Viva Insights Analyst Workbench)
- Microsoft 365 Viva Insights license — required for the organization to generate Person Query data


TWO CONNECTION OPTIONS

CSV Import (.pbit): Run query -> export CSV -> point Power BI template to the CSV file path
Direct Query (.pbit): Copy the Partition ID and Query ID from the completed query and enter them into the Power BI template — no CSV download needed; report refreshes automatically with each weekly Viva Insights update

Query must show Status = Completed before export or Direct Query connection. Do not export mid-processing.

docs.title: Admin email template — 05_Super_User_Impact_Admin_Email
docs.text:
Subject: Action Required: Viva Insights Query Setup Needed for Super User Impact Report (Power BI)

To: IT Admin / Global Admin / Viva Insights Administrator
Re: Super User Impact – Power BI Report Setup


WHAT THIS REPORT DOES

The Super User Impact Report is a Power BI report powered by Viva Insights data. Where the Super User Adoption report tracks who super users are, this report measures what changed in their work patterns as a result of Copilot adoption — including collaboration hours, meeting load, focus time, and estimated time saved. Includes cross-team benchmarking and optional sentiment signal analysis. Used to demonstrate ROI and guide change management.


DATA SOURCE REQUIRED

Viva Insights – Person Query
Export: analysis.insights.cloud.microsoft -> Create analysis -> Person query
Format: CSV or Direct Query (via Partition ID + Query ID)


REQUIRED FIELDS — DO NOT REMOVE

IMPORTANT: This report uses the same Viva Insights Person Query as the Super User Adoption report. All metric groups must be selected in full. Partial exports or manually edited CSVs with removed columns will cause blank visuals and broken calculations.

Person Query — Required Metric Groups (select "All metrics" for each):
Microsoft 365 Copilot (all metrics), Collaboration network (all metrics), Working hours collaboration (all metrics), Focus metrics (all metrics).

Specific fields the report depends on:
Copilot_Active_Use_Days_per_week, Copilot_Total_Actions, Copilot_Total_Chats, Collaboration_hours, Meeting_hours, After_hours_collaboration_hours, Email_hours, Meeting_hours_with_manager_1_on_1, Focus_hours, Uninterrupted_focus_hours, Internal_network_size, Strong_ties, Diverse_ties.

Person Query — Required Attributes (Dimensions):
PersonId, Organization, FunctionType, TimeZone, IsActive.

Query Configuration — Required Settings:
- Time period: Last 6 months (rolling)
- Group by: Week (not Month)
- Filter: Is Active = True


INSIGHTS YOU WILL GAIN

- Work pattern delta analysis: how collaboration hours, meeting time, email time, and focus time differ between super users and their peers
- After-hours work impact: whether Copilot adoption correlates with better or worse work-life balance signals
- Time saved estimation: calculated value model based on total Copilot actions
- Network changes: whether super users have broader or deeper internal collaboration networks
- Cross-team benchmarking: compare impact signals across organizations, function types, and usage tiers
- Manager 1:1 time: changes in coaching and alignment meeting patterns


ROLES & PERMISSIONS REQUIRED

Create and run Person Query in Viva Insights: Insights Analyst (assigned in Viva Insights Admin)
Access Viva Insights Analyst Workbench: Insights Analyst or Insights Administrator
Export query results as CSV: Insights Analyst


SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS

- Power BI Desktop — required to open the .pbit template file
- Access to: analysis.insights.cloud.microsoft (Viva Insights Analyst Workbench)
- Microsoft 365 Viva Insights license — required for the organization to generate Person Query data


Note:
The Super User Impact report uses the same Person Query as the Super User Adoption report. If both reports are being deployed simultaneously, a single query export can feed both templates — no need to run separate queries.

docs.title: Admin email template — 06_GitHub_Copilot_Impact_Admin_Email
docs.text:
Subject: Action Required: GitHub Data Exports Needed for GitHub Copilot Impact Report (Power BI)

To: IT Admin / GitHub Enterprise Owner / Global Admin
Re: GitHub Copilot Impact – Power BI Report Setup


WHAT THIS REPORT DOES

The GitHub Copilot Impact Report is a Power BI report that tracks how developers in your organization are building habits with GitHub Copilot. It measures weekly active users, code acceptance rates, lines of code suggested and accepted, feature adoption (code completion, agent mode, inline edits), model usage, and goal attainment by team. Designed for engineering leaders and DevOps teams to assess developer AI ROI.


DATA SOURCES REQUIRED

1. GitHub Copilot Usage Insights
   Export: GitHub Enterprise -> Insights -> Copilot Usage -> Last 28 days -> Download
   Format: JSON

2. GitHub Enterprise Members
   Export: GitHub Enterprise -> People -> Members -> CSV Report
   Format: CSV

3. Microsoft Entra ID – User/Org Data
   Export: entra.microsoft.com -> Identity -> Users -> Download users
   Format: CSV


REQUIRED FIELDS — DO NOT REMOVE

IMPORTANT: Do not pre-process, filter, or re-save the GitHub JSON or CSV exports before loading them into Power BI. Removing fields from these files will break report calculations.

GitHub Copilot Usage Insights JSON:
date, total_active_users, total_engaged_users, copilot_ide_code_completions.total_engaged_users, copilot_ide_code_completions.total_code_suggestions, copilot_ide_code_completions.total_code_acceptances, copilot_ide_code_completions.total_code_lines_suggested, copilot_ide_code_completions.total_code_lines_accepted, copilot_dotcom_chat.total_engaged_users, copilot_ide_chat.total_engaged_users, breakdown (language, editor, model arrays).

GitHub Members CSV:
login, name, email, role, organization, teams (used to link GitHub handles to Entra identity via SSO).

Microsoft Entra ID – User Export:
UserPrincipalName, Department, JobTitle.


INSIGHTS YOU WILL GAIN

- Weekly active GitHub Copilot users and engagement rate by team
- Code acceptance rate: how often developers accept AI-generated code suggestions
- Lines of code suggested vs. accepted — quantifies AI's contribution to the codebase
- Feature adoption breakdown: code completion, agent mode, inline edits
- Language-specific acceptance rates and code generation volume
- Model distribution: which AI models are contributing to the codebase
- Goal tracking: adoption target progress by team, with hotspot and bright-spot identification
- Role-level insights: whether principal engineers use agent mode at higher rates than junior developers


ROLES & PERMISSIONS REQUIRED

Export GitHub Copilot Usage Insights: GitHub Enterprise Owner
Export GitHub Enterprise Members CSV: GitHub Enterprise Owner
Export Entra user data: User Administrator or Global Reader (Entra)
Enable Copilot usage metrics policy: GitHub Enterprise Owner (must opt in under AI Controls settings)


SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS

- Power BI Desktop — required to open the .pbit template file
- GitHub Enterprise Cloud account with GitHub Copilot enabled
- Single Sign-On (SSO) enforcement between GitHub and Entra ID — required to join GitHub handle to M365 identity for org-level segmentation
- Access to: github.com/settings/enterprises, entra.microsoft.com


Prerequisite — SSO Enforcement:
The report joins GitHub user data to Entra organizational data using the SSO-linked identity. If SSO is not enforced in your GitHub Enterprise, department-level and team-level segmentation will not populate. Confirm with your GitHub Enterprise Owner that "Enforce SAML SSO" is active before exporting.

Prerequisite — Copilot Usage Metrics Policy:
GitHub Copilot usage data collection must be opted in under your enterprise's AI Controls settings. If not enabled, the usage metrics export will return empty data.

docs.title: Admin email template — 07_M365_Copilot_Readiness_Admin_Email
docs.text:
Subject: Action Required: Data Export Fields Needed for M365 Copilot Readiness Report (Power BI)

To: IT Admin / Global Admin
Re: M365 Copilot Readiness Report – Power BI Report Setup


WHAT THIS REPORT DOES

The M365 Copilot Readiness Report is a Power BI report that turns your M365 Unified Audit Log into a prioritized, tiered view of which users are ready for Copilot licensing. It scores and ranks unlicensed users by M365 app activity (Teams, Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint), classifies users into enablement strategy quadrants, and shows week-over-week engagement trends by workload. Designed for IT leaders making data-driven Copilot licensing and rollout decisions.


DATA SOURCES REQUIRED

1. Microsoft Purview – M365 Unified Audit Log
   Export: Purview portal (purview.microsoft.com) -> Audit -> Search (all activities, 90-365 day range) -> Export, or PAX PowerShell script with -IncludeM365Usage switch
   Format: CSV

2. Microsoft Entra ID – User Details
   Export: entra.microsoft.com -> Identity -> Users -> Download users, or PAX script with -IncludeUserInfo switch
   Format: CSV


REQUIRED FIELDS — DO NOT REMOVE

IMPORTANT: This report depends on specific operation types from the M365 Unified Audit Log. If you are using the PAX script, run it with the -IncludeM365Usage flag — this ensures the correct workload activity types are captured. Do not filter out operation types or remove columns from the exported file before loading into Power BI. If running a manual Purview export, do not pre-process or re-save the file. Column order and formatting must be preserved exactly as exported.

Purview Unified Audit Log — Required Columns:
CreationDate, UserIds, Operations, AuditData.

Purview Audit Log — Required Operation Types (within the export):
FileAccessed, FileModified (SharePoint/OneDrive), MessageSent (Teams/Exchange), Teams meeting events, Word activity events, Excel activity events, PowerPoint activity events, OneNote activity events.

Microsoft Entra ID – User Export:
UserPrincipalName, displayName, Department, JobTitle, hasLicense (or assignedLicenses).

Note: The hasLicense column is critical for license-based insights. The PAX script adds this automatically. If exporting manually from Entra, you must populate this column with True/False based on whether each user has a Microsoft 365 Copilot SKU assigned. Without it, the Enablement Strategy quadrant and LP Score ranking will not distinguish licensed from unlicensed users.


INSIGHTS YOU WILL GAIN

- Composite Licensing Priority (LP) score ranking every user by their M365 app activity — ready for export and stakeholder review
- Copilot Enablement Strategy: 2x2 quadrant classifying users as Enablement Targets (high M365 / low Copilot), Champions, AI-First, or Low Engagement
- 5-tier priority view: Critical, High, Medium, Promoter, Low — based on the gap between M365 engagement and Copilot adoption per app
- Week-over-week trend lines across Teams, Outlook, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
- User engagement segmentation: Daily, Frequent, Moderate, Infrequent, Inactive — cross-referenced with Copilot license status
- Department-level engagement heatmaps and cold spots


ROLES & PERMISSIONS REQUIRED

Export Purview Unified Audit Log: Audit Reader or Compliance Administrator
Export Entra user details: User Administrator or Global Reader
Run PAX script (recommended for full coverage): Audit Reader + AuditLog.Read.All, User.Read.All (Microsoft Graph)


SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS

- Power BI Desktop — required to open the .pbip template file (note: this report uses .pbip format, not .pbit)
- PowerShell 5.1+ — required only if using the PAX automated export script
- Access to: purview.microsoft.com (or compliance.microsoft.com), entra.microsoft.com


Important Notes on Export Size:
Manual Purview exports are capped at 50,000 rows (Audit Standard) or 100,000 rows (Audit Premium) per search job. For tenants with high activity or date ranges over 90 days, use the PAX PowerShell script instead — it handles pagination automatically with no row limits and can be scheduled to run unattended on a recurring basis.

Recommended Date Range:
A minimum of 90 days of audit data is required for meaningful tier ranking. Six to twelve months is recommended to produce accurate Licensing Priority scores. Shorter ranges may classify all users as "Developing" due to insufficient activity history.
